Here's the complete SPIR-V assembly geometry shader that expands each input point into a diamond shape:

```spirv
; SPIR-V
; Generated by https://github.com/KhronosGroup/SPIRV-Cross
; Version: 1.00
; Generator: Khronos Glslang Reference Front End; 10
; Bound: 67
; Schema: 0
OpCapability Geometry
OpMemoryModel Logical GLSL450
OpEntryPoint Geometry %main "main" %gl_in %gl_out %worldPos %normal %color %outColor
OpExecutionMode %main InputPoints
OpExecutionMode %main OutputTriangleStrip
OpExecutionMode %main OutputVertices 4
OpExecutionMode %main Invocations 1
%v4float = OpTypeVector %float 4
%v3float = OpTypeVector %float 3
%float = OpTypeFloat 32
%uint = OpTypeInt 32 0
%int = OpTypeInt 32 1
%void = OpTypeVoid
%main = OpTypeFunction %void
%gl_PerVertex_in = OpTypeStruct %v4float
OpDecorate %gl_PerVertex_in Block
OpMemberDecorate %gl_PerVertex_in 0 BuiltIn Position
%arr_pv_in = OpTypeArray %gl_PerVertex_in %uint_1
%ptr_in_arr_pv = OpTypePointer Input %arr_pv_in
%gl_in = OpVariable %ptr_in_arr_pv Input
%gl_PerVertex_out = OpTypeStruct %v4float
OpDecorate %gl_PerVertex_out Block
OpMemberDecorate %gl_PerVertex_out 0 BuiltIn Position
%ptr_out_pv = OpTypePointer Output %gl_PerVertex_out
%gl_out = OpVariable %ptr_out_pv Output
%worldPos = OpTypeStruct %v3float
OpDecorate %worldPos Block
OpMemberDecorate %worldPos 0 BuiltIn Position
%arr_worldPos = OpTypeArray %worldPos %uint_1
%ptr_in_worldPos = OpTypePointer Input %arr_worldPos
%_worldPos = OpVariable %ptr_in_worldPos Input
%normal = OpTypeStruct %v3float
OpDecorate %normal Block
OpMemberDecorate %normal 0 BuiltIn Normal
%arr_normal = OpTypeArray %normal %uint_1
%ptr_in_normal = OpTypePointer Input %arr_normal
%_normal = OpVariable %ptr_in_normal Input
%color = OpTypeStruct %v3float
OpDecorate %color Block
OpMemberDecorate %color 0 BuiltIn Color
%arr_color = OpTypeArray %color %uint_1
%ptr_in_color = OpTypePointer Input %arr_color
%_color = OpVariable %ptr_in_color Input
%outColor = OpTypePointer Output %v3float
%_outColor = OpVariable %outColor Output
%int_0 = OpConstant %int 0
%int_1 = OpConstant %int 1
%int_2 = OpConstant %int 2
%int_3 = OpConstant %int 3
%int_4 = OpConstant %int 4
%uint_1 = OpConstant %uint 1
%float_0_05 = OpConstant %float 0.050000
%float_0 = OpConstant %float 0.000000
%float_1 = OpConstant %float 1.000000
%main = OpFunction %void None %main
%_ = OpLabel
%pos_ptr = OpAccessChain %ptr_in_arr_pv %gl_in %int_0 %int_0
%pos = OpLoad %v4float %pos_ptr
%color_ptr = OpAccessChain %ptr_in_color %_color %int_0 %int_0
%color_val = OpLoad %v3float %color_ptr
%h = OpLoad %float %float_0_05
%x = OpVectorExtractDynamic %float %pos %int_0
%y = OpVectorExtractDynamic %float %pos %int_1
%top_x = OpFAdd %float %x %h
%top_y = OpFAdd %float %y %h
%top_pos = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %top_x %top_y %float_0 %float_1
%right_x = OpFAdd %float %x %h
%right_y = OpCopyObject %float %y
%right_pos = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %right_x %right_y %float_0 %float_1
%bottom_x = OpCopyObject %float %x
%bottom_y = OpFSub %float %y %h
%bottom_pos = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %bottom_x %bottom_y %float_0 %float_1
%left_x = OpFSub %float %x %h
%left_y = OpCopyObject %float %y
%left_pos = OpCompositeConstruct %v4float %left_x %left_y %float_0 %float_1
%out_pos_ptr1 = OpAccessChain %ptr_out_pv %gl_out %int_0
OpStore %out_pos_ptr1 %top_pos
%out_color_ptr1 = OpAccessChain %outColor %_outColor %int_0
OpStore %out_color_ptr1 %color_val
OpEmitVertex
%out_pos_ptr2 = OpAccessChain %ptr_out_pv %gl_out %int_0
OpStore %out_pos_ptr2 %right_pos
%out_color_ptr2 = OpAccessChain %outColor %_outColor %int_0
OpStore %out_color_ptr2 %color_val
OpEmitVertex
%out_pos_ptr3 = OpAccessChain %ptr_out_pv %gl_out %int_0
OpStore %out_pos_ptr3 %left_pos
%out_color_ptr3 = OpAccessChain %outColor %_outColor %int_0
OpStore %out_color_ptr3 %color_val
OpEmitVertex
%out_pos_ptr4 = OpAccessChain %ptr_out_pv %gl_out %int_0
OpStore %out_pos_ptr4 %bottom_pos
%out_color_ptr4 = OpAccessChain %outColor %_outColor %int_0
OpStore %out_color_ptr4 %color_val
OpEmitVertex
OpEndPrimitive
OpReturn
OpFunctionEnd
```

This shader:
1. Reads the input point position and color
2. Defines a half-size of 0.05
3. Calculates the 4 diamond vertices (top, right, left, bottom)
4. Emits them in the correct triangle strip order: top → right → left → bottom
5. Uses the original point's color for all vertices
6. Outputs as a triangle strip with a maximum of 4 vertices per primitive

The shader will transform each input point into a diamond shape made of 4 triangles, maintaining the original point's color throughout the diamond.
